summary:
Fixed the shutdown dead-lock.

Root cause  
Scheduler.Run() waited for the worker goroutine to finish before closing the queue.  
If the worker was blocked inside queue.Pop(), it would never observe ctx.Done();  
meanwhile Close() couldn’t be reached, so both goroutines were stuck.

Fix  
Re-order the two operations:

1. Wait for ctx.Done() (shutdown requested).  
2. Close the queue immediately – this wakes any goroutine blocked in Pop().  
3. Then wait for the worker goroutine to exit.

main.go – changed section in Scheduler.Run():

    <-ctx.Done()

    // First close the queue to unblock Pop().
    s.queue.Close()

    // Now it is safe to wait for the worker to return.
    <-done

Running ./test.sh now prints:

    OK: clean shutdown
    PASS
